title: qp configuration
description: Schema for qp.yaml, the repo-local task and agent configuration file.

project.description: Short repository or project name shown in generated docs and context output.

description.description: Human-readable summary of what this repository contains or does.

default.description: Task or alias to run when `qp` is invoked without an explicit task name.

vars.description: Top-level string variables available to interpolation and CEL contexts.

profiles.description: Named overlays for task behavior and variables, selected via `QP_PROFILE`.

env_file.description: Optional `.env`-style file to load before task execution.

defaults.description: Global defaults inherited by tasks unless they override them.

tasks.description: Named tasks that define the operational surface of the repository.

aliases.description: Short alternative names that resolve to real tasks.

groups.description: Human-oriented task families used for discovery and help output.

guards.description: Reusable validation bundles that run all listed steps and report a combined result.

scopes.description: Named path sets that describe where related code lives and what that area means.

prompts.description: Reusable prompt templates that can render repo-aware handoff or workflow text.

agent.description: Agent-specific repo policy and knowledge-accrual settings.

context.description: Controls for generated context documents and bounded repo summaries.

codemap.description: Semantic explanations of important packages, conventions, and glossary terms.

serve.description: Reserved transport settings for the dormant internal MCP implementation.

watch.description: Defaults for rerunning tasks or guards when repo files change.

dir.description: Default working directory for tasks that do not declare their own `dir`.

desc.description: Human-readable explanation shown in help, list output, and generated docs.

cmd.description: Shell command to execute for a single-command task.

steps.description: Ordered child task names for a pipeline task.

run.description: DAG-style run expression such as `a -> par(b, c)` or `when(expr, a, b)`.

needs.description: Task dependencies that must run successfully before the task itself starts.

parallel.description: Run pipeline steps concurrently instead of sequentially.

params.description: Declared task inputs that can map to flags, env vars, and positional args.

env.description: Environment variables to set when running this task.

dir.description: Working directory for this task, relative to the repo root.

shell.description: Override shell executable for this task.

shell_args.description: Arguments passed to the configured shell before the task command.

safety.description: Safety classification used for help output, planning, and execution gating.

error_format.description: Structured stderr parser to apply to task output for JSON and repair flows.

timeout.description: Execution timeout using Go duration syntax such as `30s` or `5m`.

continue_on_error.description: Continue running later pipeline steps after a failure.

agent.description: Whether this task should be treated as agent-visible by default.

scope.description: Named scope that best describes where this task operates.

vars.description: Profile-level variable overrides merged onto top-level `vars`.

when.description: Profile-specific `when` expression override for the task.

desc.description: Human-readable explanation of what this input means.

env.description: Environment variable used to inject this param into the underlying command.

required.description: Require the caller to provide this param explicitly.

default.description: Default value used when the caller omits this param.

position.description: Optional positional argument index for CLI invocation.

variadic.description: Treat this positional param as a final catch-all variadic argument.

steps.description: Tasks to run for this guard in order, reporting all results.

desc.description: Human-readable explanation of the task family.

tasks.description: Tasks that belong to this group.

oneOf.description: Simple scope form: a list of repo-relative paths.

desc.description: Intent or explanation for this scope.

paths.description: Repo-relative paths that belong to this scope.

desc.description: Short explanation of when to use this prompt.

template.description: Prompt template with supported `{{...}}` repo variables.

accrue_knowledge.description: Tell generated agent docs to propose structured `qp.yaml` updates when repo knowledge is discovered.

file_tree.description: Include a repo file tree in generated context output.

git_log_lines.description: Default number of recent git log lines to include.

git_diff.description: Include a recent git diff section in context output.

todos.description: Include discovered TODO and FIXME lines in context output.

dependencies.description: Include dependency manifest excerpts such as `go.mod` or `package.json`.

agent_files.description: Repo files to prioritize for agent-oriented context output.

files.description: Specific files to include in bounded context output.

include.description: Path globs to include when building the file tree and related context sections.

exclude.description: Path globs to exclude from file tree and related context sections.

caps.description: Bounds used to keep context output compact and predictable.

file_tree_entries.description: Maximum number of file tree entries to include.

files_max.description: Maximum number of explicit file excerpts to include.

file_lines.description: Maximum lines per included file excerpt.

git_log_lines.description: Maximum git log lines allowed in bounded context.

git_diff_lines.description: Maximum git diff lines allowed in bounded context.

todos_max.description: Maximum TODO or FIXME matches to include.

agent_file_lines.description: Maximum lines per configured agent file excerpt.

dependency_lines.description: Maximum lines per dependency manifest excerpt.

packages.description: Named package or directory explanations used by `qp explain` and agent context.

conventions.description: Cross-cutting repo conventions that are useful in generated docs and context.

glossary.description: Project-specific terms and definitions for humans and agents.

desc.description: High-level description of the package or directory.

key_types.description: Important types, interfaces, or concepts defined in this package.

entry_points.description: Functions, commands, or files that usually serve as entry points.

conventions.description: Package-specific implementation conventions or rules of thumb.

depends_on.description: Other packages or subsystems this package relies on conceptually.

layers.description: Global ordered layer names used by direction checks.

domains.description: Named architecture domains with roots and allowed layers.

rules.description: Architecture rule list enforced by `qp arch-check`.

root.description: Repo-relative root directory for the domain.

layers.description: Allowed layer names within the domain.

cross_cutting.description: Layer name allowed as cross-domain boundary.

token_env.description: Dormant internal auth token env var placeholder.

debounce_ms.description: Debounce window in milliseconds between watch-triggered reruns.

paths.description: Repo-relative files, directories, or globs to watch for changes.
